data.narrative:
Reaction Dynamics is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Bachar Elzein that has raised C$38M to date. The valuation assessment draws from a peer set of five same-sector, same-or-adjacent-stage private companies: Aalyria ($1.3B, Series B), Stoke Space ($1.0B, Series B), ClearSpace ($400M, Series A), Hubble Network ($400M, Series B), and Sift ($274M, Series B). This peer universe reflects the broader competitive landscape of eight private and five public space and aerospace companies.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach, given the target's private status and undisclosed revenue. The median valuation across the five comparables is $400M, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. This establishes a reasonable anchor for a Series A company operating in a sector where late-stage rounds command significant valuations. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 45.3x across five public tickers including RKLB and PL) cannot be applied without the target's revenue data.

The triangulated valuation range is $340M to $460M, with a median of $400M. The primary uncertainty is the tightness of comparable data: only one methodology is applicable, and peer valuations span a wide range reflecting heterogeneous business models and stage progression. Stage-appropriate discounting relative to Series B comps may warrant positioning toward the lower end of the range absent concrete revenue or growth metrics.
